name: Hampworth
overview: Hamptworth (usually spelt Hamptworth, though recorded here as Hampworth) is a hamlet in the civil parish of Landford in the extreme south-east of Wiltshire, within the New Forest National Park, about 10 miles south-east of Salisbury. First mentioned in the early 13th century, it lies among woodland once part of the Royal Forest of Melchet; Manor Farm dates from the 15th century and Hamptworth Lodge is an Arts and Crafts country house of 1912. Until a 2017 boundary change it was part of Redlynch parish.
character: A dispersed, heavily wooded New Forest-edge hamlet of farmsteads, cottages and country houses spread along quiet lanes and commons.
amenities: The Cuckoo Inn (historic pub - check current opening, as it has frequently been closed)
The New Forest CE Primary School nearby at Nomansland/Landford
No village shop - everyday shopping is at Landford or Downton

schools.name: The New Forest Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 10 miles north-west, partly via the A36, for shops, services and the station
Via the A36 to the south-east
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m

prices.summary: Stock is mainly larger detached houses, farmsteads and country properties in a wooded setting, in low volumes; the New Forest National Park location and golf-course proximity support premium values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking, cycling and riding in the New Forest National Park and former Melchet forest woodland
Exploring the nearby New Forest commons and the Wiltshire-Hampshire border country
pros: Secluded, wooded New Forest National Park setting
Characterful period and country houses with privacy and space
cons: Very dispersed with no village shop
The Cuckoo Inn has frequently been closed
Car essential; National Park planning constraints apply
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a secluded, wooded New Forest-edge hamlet with golf and countryside, who value privacy and space over having amenities on the doorstep.

sources.label: Ofsted - The New Forest CE Primary School (URN 148024, Good, Dec 2024)
